content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 8, 1992

Palmer Brown Madden
McCutchen, Doyle, Brown & Enersen
1331 No. California Blvd.
P.O. Box V
Walnut Creek, CA  94596

Re:  Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-92-242

Dear Mr. Palmer:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹  Because you have not
provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you
are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for
informal assistance.²

Your letter, dated April 13, 1992, accurately summarizes my
telephone advice to you that an initiative becomes a measure when
the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions to qualify
the measure for the ballot.  The costs incurred before an initia-
tive is in circulation are not reportable events.  These costs may
include attorneys' fees incurred while preparing a draft of the
initiative, political consulting, voter polling about the proposed
text, and the voluntary time of an attorney used for delivery of
the prepared initiative to a qualification committee.  If a pre-
circulation poll is given to a committee for use in its qualifica-
tion effort, the fair market value of the poll is a reportable
contribution.

____________

1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Palmer Brown Madden
I-92-242
Page 2


     Your letter also correctly summarizes my advice that if a
consultant is hired on the basis of "payment only if successful,"
the fair market value of the consultant's time is reportable as a
non-monetary (in-kind) loan, on the campaign report covering the
period in which the services are received.  For your convenience,
I have enclosed advice memorandum M-83-165, which will provide you
with the proper method for reporting such "in-kind loans" by both
the recipient and the contributor.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                   By:  Sandra L. Taylor
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting requirements for costs and contributions related to initiative campaigns, including pre-circulation expenses, polls, and consultants hired on a contingency basis?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An initiative becomes a reportable measure when signature circulation begins; pre-circulation costs (legal fees, polling, consulting) are not reportable unless a poll is given to a committee for qualification work (then fair market value is reportable); consultants hired on a 'payment only if successful' basis must report their fair market value as an in-kind loan.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter (no immunity provided); no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but substantive Q&A content present in body; references enclosed memorandum N-83-165 for reporting guidance; document date May 8, 1992; requestor did not provide client identity, triggering informal assistance classification

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting requirements for costs and contributions related to initiative campaigns, including pre-circulation expenses, polls, and consultants hired on a contingency basis?

CONCLUSION: An initiative becomes a reportable measure when signature circulation begins; pre-circulation costs (legal fees, polling, consulting) are not reportable unless a poll is given to a committee for qualification work (then fair market value is reportable); consultants hired on a 'payment only if successful' basis must report their fair market value as an in-kind loan.

Box V Walnut Creek, CA 94596 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-92-242 Dear Mr. Palmer: This is in response to your request for confirmation of telephone advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because you have not provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance.² Your letter, dated April 13, 1992, accurately summarizes my telephone advice to you that an initiative becomes a measure when the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions to qualify the measure for the ballot. The costs incurred before an initia- tive is in circulation are not reportable events. These costs may include attorneys' fees incurred while preparing a draft of the initiative, political consulting, voter polling about the proposed text, and the voluntary time of an attorney used for delivery of the prepared initiative to a qualification committee. If a pre- circulation poll is given to a committee for use in its qualifica- tion effort, the fair market value of the poll is a reportable contribution. ____________ 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. For your convenience, I have enclosed advice memorandum M-83-165, which will provide you with the proper method for reporting such "in-kind loans" by both the recipient and the contributor. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure requirements for initiatives, clarifying that pre-circulation costs are generally not reportable, but certain polls and contingency-based consultant services have reportable fair market values.
